title: Inventory Carrying Cost Calculator
meta_description: Calculate the annual cost of holding inventory. Enter your carrying cost rate and average inventory value to see total carrying cost, with the formula explained.

inputs.label: Carrying cost rate
inputs.help: Annual carrying cost as a fraction of value, e.g. 0.25 for 25%. Typical range 15-30%.

inputs.label: Average inventory value
inputs.help: Average value of inventory held during the year, at cost.

result_cards.label: Annual carrying cost
result_cards.description: Estimated annual cost of holding this inventory.

copy_blocks.intro: Carrying cost is what it costs to hold inventory for a year: capital tied up, storage, insurance, shrinkage, and obsolescence. It is usually expressed as a percentage of inventory value.
copy_blocks.how_it_works: Multiply your carrying cost rate by the average value of inventory held. If you do not know your rate, derive it as annual carrying costs divided by average inventory value.
copy_blocks.formula_explanation: Annual carrying cost = carrying cost rate x average inventory value. The rate bundles capital, storage, service, and risk costs and is policy-dependent.
copy_blocks.worked_example: At a 26% carrying rate on 2,000,000 of average inventory, annual carrying cost = 0.26 x 2,000,000 = 520,000.

faq.a: Many firms use 15-30% depending on capital cost, storage, and risk. Derive your own from annual carrying costs divided by average inventory value where possible.
Cost of capital, warehousing and handling that varies with volume, insurance, taxes, shrinkage, damage, and obsolescence.

faq.q: How does this connect to EOQ?
faq.a: The carrying rate times unit cost is the per-unit holding cost the EOQ formula uses to balance against ordering cost.
